tools.name: get_schema
tools.description: Get database schema information including tables and columns

table_name.description: Optional: specific table name to get schema for

tools.name: query_data
tools.description: Query data from the database (SELECT only for safety)

query.description: SQL query to execute (SELECT statements only)

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: List all tables in the database

tools.name: describe_table
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific table including indexes and constraints

table_name.description: Name of the table to describe

tools.name: get_table_sample
tools.description: Get a sample of rows from a table

table_name.description: Name of the table to sample

limit.description: Number of rows to return (default: 10, max: 100)

tools.name: check_certificate_cache
tools.description: Check the status of the AWS RDS certificate cache

tools.name: update_data
tools.description: Update rows in a table with specified values and conditions

table_name.description: Name of the table to update

values.description: Object with column names as keys and new values (alias: 'data')

data.description: Alias for 'values' — object with column names as keys and new values

where.description: Object with column names as keys and values to match for WHERE clause

dry_run.description: If true, only preview what would be updated without executing

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to update (safety limit)

tools.name: delete_data
tools.description: Delete rows from a table based on specified conditions

table_name.description: Name of the table to delete from

where.description: Object with column names as keys and values to match for WHERE clause

dry_run.description: If true, only preview what would be deleted without executing

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to delete (safety limit)

tools.name: insert_data
tools.description: Insert new rows into a table

table_name.description: Name of the table to insert into

data.description: Object with column names as keys and values to insert

tools.name: execute_raw_query
tools.description: Execute any SQL query including INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE (use with caution)

params.description: Optional array of parameters for parameterized queries

tools.name: count_rows
tools.description: Count rows in a table with optional WHERE conditions

table_name.description: Name of the table to count rows from

where.description: Optional: Object with column names as keys and values to match for WHERE clause

tools.name: table_exists
tools.description: Check if a table exists in the database

table_name.description: Name of the table to check

tools.name: column_exists
tools.description: Check if a column exists in a table

column_name.description: Name of the column to check

tools.name: get_relationships
tools.description: Get foreign key relationships for tables

table_name.description: Optional: specific table name to get relationships for

tools.name: create_table
tools.description: Create a new table with specified columns

table_name.description: Name of the table to create
Name of the table to alter

tools.name: get_connection_status
tools.description: Get database connection status and performance metrics

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 14 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
